text: Abstract: This study investigated the religious values embodied by students through a Culture of Faithful Friday (Jum’at Beriman) and its influence towards strengthening the students’ character. Based on the concept of the Culture of Faithful Friday (Jum’at Beriman), the educational values of religion which are enlivened through Friday atmosphere should be known and mastered by students, so that it is able to support an educational process aimed at fostering students’ character in SMK. The research design employed in this study is qualitative field research using the case study method by collecting data from school leaders, teachers, and students. The results of this study showed that activities enlivened in SMK are reading the Quran and performing congregational prayer in the morning (Dhuha) and Tahlil and Mujahadah. In line with the philosophy of culture of Faithful Friday, these religious activities aim at disseminating religious values relating to aqidah (faith), ibadah (worship), and kearifan (wisdom). As a result, a number of characters such as students’ faithfulness, awareness towards social, environmental, peace, peace, accountability, and leadership are built and strengthening students’ character, therefore bridging the normative values of religion in schools into students’ actions both in and out of class. Hence, SMK can become a role model in terms of nurturing character through vocational education activities at schools.
explanation: Moderate likelihood: the text uses plausible academic phrasing and domain terms, with minor repetition and phrasing oddities that can appear in translated or AI-generated text, but nothing definitively signals AI.
